us.label: US Wedding

timeline.label: Ceremony
timeline.description: Join us under the arbor as we exchange our vows
timeline.details: The ceremony will take place in the Rose Garden, weather permitting. In case of rain, we'll move to the Conservatory (indoor). Please arrive by 6:15 PM to find your seat. Phones are welcome but we ask that you stay seated and refrain from blocking the aisle.

timeline.label: Cocktail Hour
timeline.description: Hors d'oeuvres and drinks in the garden courtyard
timeline.details: Enjoy passed hors d'oeuvres featuring seasonal ingredients from the garden's own farm. A selection of wine, beer, and our signature cocktail will be served. This is a great time to explore the garden courtyard and take photos with the golden hour light.

timeline.label: Reception
timeline.description: Dinner, toasts, and dancing under the stars
timeline.details: A plated dinner will be served followed by heartwarming toasts from our closest family and friends. After dinner, the dance floor opens! We've curated a playlist spanning classics, Motown, and modern hits. The bar will remain open throughout. Late-night snacks will appear around 10 PM.

us.dressCode: Formal / Black-tie optional — garden party attire welcome, just no white please

transport.car: The New York Botanical Garden is located at 2900 Southern Blvd, Bronx, NY. There are several parking garages nearby.
transport.train: Metro-North Railroad from Grand Central to Botanical Garden Station. The journey takes approximately 20 minutes.
transport.subway: Take the 2 or 5 train to Pelham Parkway station, then a short taxi or bus ride to the garden.

india.label: India Wedding

timeline.label: Ceremony
timeline.details: Details about the Indian wedding ceremony will be shared as the date approaches. The ceremony will follow traditional Hindu customs including the jaimala (garland exchange), saptapadi (seven steps), and sindoor.

timeline.label: Reception
timeline.details: A grand dinner reception with live music, dancing, and a feast of traditional Indian cuisine. More details to come.

india.dressCode: Traditional Indian attire encouraged — sarees, lehengas, and kurta-pajamas welcome
